data.title: Data suggests Ether could rally to $2.8K. However, ETH futures data shows a divided market, limiting odds for 33% jump.
data.description: Bullish investors in Ether are aiming for a price target of $2,800, but data from ETH futures indicates a market split with low chances of a continuous 33% increase.

data.title: Notice of Data Security Incident
data.description: New York-based Daniel H. Cook Associates (DHCA) recently disclosed a data security breach potentially involving patient or client information. The incident occurred on October 17, 2025, when DHCA faced a network disruption. The exact number of individuals affected by this breach remains undisclosed. DHCA is actively investigating the matter to assess the extent of the breach and provide necessary support to those impacted.

data.title: BLUE OWL CAPITAL CORPORATION II BOARD UNANIMOUSLY RECOMMENDS SHAREHOLDERS REJECT UNSOLICITED MINORITY OFFER FROM COX AND SABA
data.description: Blue Owl Capital Corporation II, also known as OBDC II, declared on March 13, 2026, that its Board of Directors has proposed a discounted offer which is significantly below the OBDC II NAV. This decision overlooks the substantial capital return already in progress.

data.title: BlackRock® Canada Announces Product Changes
data.description: In Toronto on March 13, 2026, BlackRock Asset Management Canada Limited, a subsidiary of BlackRock, Inc., disclosed modifications to three funds. BlackRock is listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol BLK.

data.title: Claude 4.6 Models Reach 1M Tokens, Long-Context Premium Removed at Standard API Cost.
data.description: Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 have upgraded to provide complete 1 million token context windows at regular API rates, removing the additional cost for long-context use. This enhancement allows users to access extended context without any additional fees.

data.title: CPI Inflation In Line As Rent Slows, Energy Heats Up; S&P 500 Falls
data.description: Inflation would likely be on track to meet its target if it weren't for the impact of the conflict in Iran. The latest Consumer Price Index (CPI) indicates that inflation is stable, with rents showing a decrease in growth while energy prices are rising. Additionally, the S&P 500 index has experienced a drop in response to these developments.

data.title: InflaRx Announces Receipt of Nasdaq Deficiency Notice Regarding Minimum Bid Price Requirement
data.description: JENA, Germany - On March 13, 2026, InflaRx N.V., a leading biopharmaceutical company trading on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol IFRX, revealed that it has officially been notified by The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC regarding non-compliance with the Minimum Bid Price Rule. The notice, dated March 11, 2026, specified that for the past thirty consecutive business days, the bid price of the company's ordinary shares had finished below the stipulated minimum requirement of $1.00 per share for continued listing on Nasdaq under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5450(a)(1). This notification raises concerns about the company's compliance with Nasdaq listing standards.

data.title: Peyto Exploration & Development Corp. Confirms Monthly Dividend for April 15, 2026
data.description: Peyto Exploration & Development Corp., based in Calgary, Alberta, announced on March 13, 2026, that they will be paying a monthly dividend of $0.11 per common share in March 2026. Shareholders of record on March 31, 2026, will receive the dividend on April 15, 2026. The company is traded on the Toronto Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol PEY.

data.title: Jayud Global Logistics Announces Pricing of $6.7 Million Registered Direct Offering
data.description: Shenzhen-based company Jayud Global Logistics Limited (NASDAQ: JYD) has disclosed a recent development on March 13, 2026, revealing its agreement with multiple investors for the acquisition and trade of 5,025,000 Class A ordinary shares. These shares were sold at $1.34 per share in a registered direct offering, generating an estimated total of $6.73 million in gross proceeds for the company. Jayud is known for its expertise in cross-border logistics as a prominent provider of end-to-end supply chain solutions.

data.title: Austin jury finds former executives misappropriated ES3 Minerals trade secrets, awards more than $49 million in damages
data.description: The recent two-week trial in the Texas Business Court has ended with a unanimous verdict. The case involved allegations against former senior executives who were accused of using stolen proprietary systems to establish a competing mineral brokerage. This verdict was reached in Austin, Texas, on March 13, 2026. The company at the center of the dispute, ES3 Minerals, LLC, is based in Austin. The outcome of the trial marks a significant milestone in this legal battle and underscores the serious nature of the allegations.
New stock trades disclosed by Austin Scott: GEV, GE.
